reviews.review_msg: goated prof

Get ready for this course to wreck your gpa and pride. The TAs feel entirely useless but peter himself seems like an awesome guy. He allowed me to redo a paper my ta marked me POORLY on, that said ta set me up for failure for. This redo changed the letter grade overnight and I am in deep gratitude for dr Cramer. I have lots of stories.

For an English course there wasn't a whole lot of reading, which I was grateful for. Cramer is also just a really nice guy and super approachable. My TA for this class was also excellent. I found the assignments were a little complicated to understand but I'd take them any day over a final. There was no final, no midterm which was awesome.
Lectures are long, assignments can be so hard understand from the instructions.

a little dry, but he does his best. very knowledgeable and accessible outside of class. he does talk a lot tho. he didn't let me have an extension after my dad died bc of the course policy, which rlly messed with my head, but i guess i can understand.
My favourite prof that I've had so far. Class is fun as long you participate. If you keep up with the readings and attend class there is no reason you can't get a decent grade in his classes

Dry, but not a bad professor. The lectures are 3 hours long, and most of class is based on discussion on concepts that are difficult to understand sometimes. His rubrics are extremely vague and difficult to adhere to.

Dr. Cramer is a wonderful professor who listens to his student's concerns and welcomes questions about course material. Take a rhetoric class with him if you can! It is super useful for future English classes and 3 hour seminars are fun as long as you make an effort to participate.

Peter's the most humble and down-to-earth prof I've ever met. The class content and readings are difficult, but he takes the time to walk us through and listen to everyone's input during discussions. His classes are an absolute delight. Take the time to go to his office hours, too.

214 had very dry readings and lectures could be quite boring and lengthy. However, I feel this course was very helpful and I learned a lot. Would definitely take again. Peter is a fair marker and kind to his students. Doing the readings before class and participating is a must.

Cramer is a nice guy, though his lecture is quite boring. Unfortunately, you must show up to his class to pass. The class is graded on 3 papers, the rubrics provided online are vague, and he briefly explains the structures of these papers in class. The SFU course page he uses barely works. AVOID.

Peter is fantastic. Super accessible and caring -- you can tell that he genuinely wants his students to succeed. The material can be a little dense, especially if you're not interested in rhetoric, but I personally found his courses to be interesting. I would definitely recommend!

I have taken multiple courses with Professor Cramer and I honestly do not understand why he has negative reviews. He motivates me to further my knowledge and is always a pleasure to listen to in lecture. His focus is on Rhetoric so if you have no interest in the subject you might find his courses difficult. Amazing professor over all though

Takes the time to thoroughly explain concepts and make sure students understand. He doesn't lecture in the traditional sense and puts more of an emphasis on class discussions, but overall is a very fair marker, helpful and a great instructor to have. Definitely one of the easier 400 level instructors.

If you can power through material that can drag, youll come out with a decent mark. He's a super helpful guy that wants everyone to succeed. 2 Papers and a midterm, which is the easiest multiple choice test I've taken. If you're looking for an easier 4th year english course, look for Peter. The material he teaches takes some time to figure out..

If your desire is to develop your writing skills then taking a class with Peter is a must. If you want inspiring lectures, then maybe stick to Everton and Budra; Peter's teaching style is different. He's a very smart guy and genuinely wants you to succeed, but you have to put in the work. His office hours are very useful and he's easy to talk to.

Peter is monotone, but is silly and the stuff he laughs is hilarious. He is a very easy marker since he believes in subjectivity and as long as you have a good argument or evidence, you will do great! He is also very good in office hours, and will help as much as he can. Overall, a great prof and seems like a pretty chill guy. I'd chill with him

Here's the thing. I found this course easy. Others? Not so much. I don't blame them. It's difficult to soak in knowledge when the lecturer's voice sounds like the lullaby I'll play to my future child to put him/her to sleep. As if that wasn't enough, the man paces around like a pendulum in a further attempt to hypnotize students into hibernation.

Unclear grading scheme. He doesn't try to help students succeed. Things learned in this class are completely useless. It's a 100 level english course yet we're learning about genre analysis and scientific writing. Course material was difficult to understand. Lectures are boring enough to put an insomniac to sleep.
I like him, but I hate his lecture
This course could make sense if someone else was teaching it.

The most boring lecture I have ever attended in all of university. Peter's voice put me to sleep every class. The papers have no structure and grading is never clear. I did not learn anything about english nor did I improve my writing skills. 114 is about analyzing scientific writing- not what the description says on canvas. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS

Peter Cramers lectures are extremely boring and he is very monotone. He doesn't seem to like what he's teaching which makes it very dull. Assignments don't make sense. Definitely wouldn't take another class with him ever. Do not recommend.

Peter has a great teaching style that really encourages you to participate in order to learn.  The material can be quite difficult to grasp at times, but he goes in depth and makes sure you really understand it.  He's a genuinely nice guy too and really seems to care about his students.

Peter definitely has an interest in Rhetoric and engages in his lectures with it. Some of it can be  dry if you aren't very interested in it, but he's all-in-all great. Papers are weird to write at first, it's definitely a learning experience; concision is required like in Philosophy. Two fair papers and a mid-term with NO final, which is great.

grading scheme promotes success since your grade comes from participation, quiz based on lecture and readings, and two essays.  discourse analysis is totally different and a rewarding subject once you get the hang of it.  totally changed how I look at discourse.  Cramer's a funny guy and really helpful & available.  Totally recommend!
Aproachable, helpul, friendly & informative. Engaging and unique teaching tactics,  Distracting smile& Good sense of humor. not a bad way to spend a couple in-class hours

Peter Cramer made my undergrad twice, maybe three times as good as it would have been without him. The courses I took with him have informed my ways of thinking, reading and writing. I agree with a previous reviewer on the point of his distracting smile and good looks. If you are interested in the English language, his courses are not to be missed.
Peter is the best professor I've ever taken a class with. Very easy to approach with questions. I actually found him easy and pleasurable to listen to, but maybe that's just me... Highly recommend.

Probably two 50 minutes sessions for lecture is not best suited to his teaching style: it never seemed long enough. He was flexible about the syllabus schedule to accommodate discussion that pushed the lecture content forward. Good class, really great Prof.

Class was a bit dull, however at the end of semester it got a bit better. There were no notes so if you didnt go to class you would probably struggle in knowing what he wanted u to focus on in plato. assignments were not grades very fairly and i felt like they had nothing to do with english or plato for that matter

So boring that I realized I could be doing something better with my life than attending his lecture... like counting all the teeth in my mouth with my tongue, or picking up my brain that has oozed onto the floor due to extreme and painful boredom. If you attend class, bring a magazine.

I found his good looks and smile really distracting in class. Class heavily relies on discussion, which is fun. He has a tendency to talk at length sometimes to a rambling point, and class attention would wane. Overall, fantastic, recommended for his Rhetoric class. He's a fair marker, clear about his expectations, and fun.

Peter Cramer is an intelligent man but one of the worst lectures I've had. The content could be interesting yet he presents it in a very toned down and dismissive manner. Despite the fact that this class was entitled "Dialogue and Decision Making" there was little dialogue encouraged and when there was he didn't seem to know how to deal with it.

Peter is clearly passionate about the subject, but his lecture style isn't high energy, so don't expect that. It is geared toward class discussion, which is helpful. The readings are relevant and the papers can be sizable, but not difficult. Very helpful, approachable, clear and fair. Marking isn't easy, nor is it tough. Also kind of sexy.
AWESOME TEACHER!!!! The entire class is discussion, so if you don't like talking don't take this class! He is the BEST teacher at SFU! He actually makes English interesting!! Best class!!

Cramer is awesome... in our class he enouraged a lot of class discussion, so sometimes there were long, awkward silences in class until someone piped up... but the topic of Verbal Hygiene is so interesting. I think he tried to stretch the topic over a whole semester when there could have been other topics covered, but it made it easy to study.
Awesome professor. Helpful, gives good feedback on essays and makes seminars fun. I'd recommend anyone to take his upper level rhetoric class. :)

Even though I have a 3.7 GPA, I still learned so much by taking this course. Peter sometimes takes things a bit slowly, but overall he's a fabulous instructor. He's also a tough marker so be prepared to work hard. But he's enthusiastic when students show interest. He is a very nice person, and also very nice to look at.
Cramer is a good and helpful prof. He marks essays hard, but one's writing skills improve significantly after taking his class. He is very approachable. Yes, a bit monotone, and loves drinking tea.

he's easy going/nice & i find this course really interesting! not a difficult course, all u have to do is to attend lectures, write essays & study the notes... textbooks are extremely useless (i've never read a page)... lectures can be quite boring but they r helpful/clear/prepared & hint: study ALL the notes (time & date & name) & do practices
He has pretty good notes which provide you with the basics you need to write the papers. His lectures can be quite boring, but the powerpoint slides tell it all.
Really great, yes a bit monotone, but he is fair and helpful.

Good prof. Material is intersting but lectures are VERY boring. No point to go to lecture. Tutorials are very helpful/where u get your marks. Prof is monotone but a very nice guy and easy going. Final exam/paper grading=VERY fair!
Material is interesting , but the lectures are very boring. Cramer is a nice guy but is very monotone.
This class rocked. The prof's note were all you needed and he was very helpful during office hours. i would take another class with this prof anyday.... best class thus far
Class and Prof are ok, Peter's notes are pretty much all you need, the textbooks have too much other info in them. Pretty easy class...
Classes are boring , not able to explain some of the materials , question and answers are off topic , leaves a generally broad feeling of answers
text is useless, material is irrelevant to course description, and he once " assaulted students by throwing an orange at the lecturees"
what a waste. :(

I really enjoyed the MPub program at SFU, but this class was boring. The lack of clarity of ideas was hard to get through, even though I did pretty well. I do wish, however,  that there were less "comments" about how bad it is in the tech writing field. It makes those of us in it really uncomfortable.

An OK instructor. Easy-going and nice, though needs work on clarity and maybe explaining more about subject matter...sometimes confusing. This class was super boring though. Not what I expected at all from reading the course description.
